text:
Mom!

Look at this, Mom!

And mine. Cool, isn't it?

Ed and Al...

I'm so proud.

Your alchemy skills have improved!

Will Daddy be impressed?

He'll be back one day so you can show him.

Yippee!

Watch out, you two!

Mom?

Mom!

I'm hungry.

And cold.

I want to see Mom...

The alchemy book had something about the Homunculi.

You mean artificial humans?

I wonder if we can bring Mom back.

But it's taboo for alchemists to create humans.

We don't need to tell anyone.

35 liters of water, 20 kilograms of carbon.

4 liters of ammonia, 1.5 kilograms of lime.

800 grams of phosphorus, 250 grams of salt.

100 grams of saltpeter, 80 grams of sulfur.

7.5 grams of fluorine, 5 grams of iron.

3 grams of silicon... Plus 15 other chemicals.

Adult humans are made of... readily available products.

All in line with the Law of Equivalent Exchange... of alchemy.

We need one more thing.

The data of a human soul.

Is this really okay?

Don't say that now!

But in the alchemy book it says it's taboo.

Don't you want to see Mom again?

I do...

Okay then!

Ready, Al?

Okay.

Something is wrong!

Al!

Ed!

Al!

Ed!

Ed!

Al!

Ed!

Ed, help!

Al!

Old man, just hand me that ring.

The Philosopher's Stone!

Give it up!

That's awesome!

The Stone does that?

It must be the real thing.

You can transmute without a circle!

You saved my ass!

You're hollow!

What are you two?

Hey.

That hurts.

Get off me!

That... prosthetic arm...

and leg!

You're... the Fullmetal Alchemist!

You mean you've heard of me?

Now hand it over.

The Philosopher's Stone!

How sneaky...

His Holiness? His Holiness?

Don't you see?

The pastor doesn't perform miracles.

He conned everyone in town. He's a fake.

Stay away!

I'll k*ll her!

You third-grade alchemist!

I'll show you what separates us.

Huh?

Focus!

It's been a long time, Fullmetal.

Colonel!

The Flame Alchemist.

Arrest him!

What? Let me go!

I came to town for an inspection when I heard a puny alchemist was causing havoc.

Who are you calling puny?

Hey!

What in the hell have you made?

Let go!

I finally found the Stone.

The Philosopher's Stone is perfect and indestructible...

Correct?

It's another fake...

Ed.

Sorry, Al.

You!

Halt!

Take him to East Area Headquarters. Yes, sir!

Ed.

Hey nowI You must be  the famous Fullmetal Alchemist!

Oh, wow. You became a State-certified alchemist when you were just  12!

That armor that you wear. That must be how you got your nickname. 

Alphonse: I think you must mean my brother. Not me.

You mean, the short guy?

Alphonse: Yes.

No way.

Alphonse: But It's true! My brother is so good that he can perform alchemy without drawing a transmutation circle. Sure he might be short but he has a big heart.

Well, whoever. What I want to know is. Can you ask your shrimpy brother to replace my food stail with one that’s made of marble?

Alphonse: Alchemy is not magic. It's science. We can't do that...

Don't be stingy. Come on. Like this!

Alphonse: Alchemy follows the Law of Equivalent Exchange. You can only materialize things of the same value. It also follows the conservation of mass laws and natural providence. Then there’s the three major elements and the four protyles.  Ah... Okay. If I had one potion of something, I can only create the same amount of something else. If I had water, I would only be able to create something that was similar in nature.

Nothing you say makes sense, but in short... you can't make marble out of wood?

Alphonse: I'm really sorry.

Winry: Al!

Alphonse: Winry!

Winry: It's been so long. Where's Ed?

Edward: I'll find it no matter what!

Mustang: Listen to me. The Philosopher's Stone is just a myth.

Edward: Yeah? Well, what about the red stone? That wasn’t the work of standard alchemy.

Mustang: Like I already told you... the stone was an amplifier. The military has also researched how to amplify alchemy. To disappointing results. Twice, maybe three times greater. How much would you need for human transmutation? A thousand times? Ten thousand? Regardless. The state denies the existence of the Philosopher's Stone.

Edward: The military would love to believe that!

Alphonse: What's that? You’re a State Alchemist . Would you defy its position?

Edward: Without the Philosopher's Stone, Al will never be able to get his body back. I became a state alchemist so I could access valuable information about the Philosopher's Stone. I’ll be the military's dog as long as I can get Al’s body.

Mustang: And find a way to recover your lost limbs.

Edward: Don’t you get it?? I don’t care about that! Al comes first. I only wanted to get my brother’s body back.

Mustang: And you would destroy a whole city in the pursuit of this goal.

Edward: I would do anything to get Al’s body back.

Mustang: Whenever the reason, your duty is to follow orders. My advice to you. Forget about the philosophers stone and explore other options.

Hughes: Excuse me, Colonel. Hey!

Edward: Captain Hughes!

Hughes: You’re looking good, Edward.

Mustang: General Hakuro.

Hakuro: So you’re the Fullmetal Alchemist. I’ve been looking forward to meeting you. I heard you’ve been causing some chaos lately. And what about your younger brother in armor?

Edward: He's fixing up the town right now.

Hakuro: He’s picking up after you! I'd love to meet your armored brother of yours. I’d also love to hear about your adventures some time.

Edward: Yeah!

Edward: "Whenever the reason your duty is to follow orders." He acts so superior... Everytime I meet the Colonel, he’s more obnoxious.

Hughes: We joined the military at the same time. But I guess he has what it takes to get promoted to command.

Edward: And are you saying you don’t captain?

Hughes: Hey! Watch it.

Edward: So why are you at Eastern headquarters?

Hughes: I wasn’t demoted if that’s what you’re thinking. I’m just here for an inspection. I just got in from Central City and I am beat!

Winry: Edward!

Edward: Winry?

Winry: Are you kidding me?! Do you know how much time I put into this top notch automail arm?

Edward: Sorry.

Winry: What did you do this time to ruin it like this?

Edward: I don’t know. There was this stone dog. 

Winry: A what! This is beyond repair. I'll have to make a brand new one.

Hakuro: Your peer showed up without notice from central.

Mustang: You’re here for an inspection? Right this minute?

Hakuro: Yes, I am. 

Mustang: Pardon me asking, but do you have an ulterior motive?

Hakuro: Do you have a reason to think I do? As you know, The Führer works in mysterious ways. I expect great things from you, young man. But watch your back. Elites like you are easy targets. Shou Tucker. Have you ever heard of him?

Mustang: Yes. Isn’t he the alchemist who specialized in transmuting chimeras?

Hakuro: That’s Tucker, yes. Could you introduce the brothers to him?

Mustang: You mean Ed and Al, sir?

Hakuro: I want to do something to help those boys.

Mustang: Affirmative, sir.

Hey! Wait a minute. I still have those townspeople in the palm of my hand! Listen! Everything got screwed up because of your Philosopher's Stone which was a fake!

Lust: A fake, you say? Do you think there's a real Philosopher's Stone? Unless there was a real one, there couldn’t be a fake one.  Right, Envy?

Lust: Look at that. Now there’s no real Cornello.

Envy: Well, then, I suppose I can’t be called the fake one then.

Gluttony: Hey, Lust... Can I eat this, please?

Lust: Not this one, Gluttony. If you eat this one... He’ll upset your tummy.

Winry: Miss Gracia, are you sure it’s okay for us to stay the night?

Gracia: Be my guest. The more the merrier.

Hughes: We’re here for a month. But the house is huge for us.

Edward: So the military wastes money and resources on captains, huh?

Hughes: Winry, as a mechanic, I bet you have all kinds of trouble with this guy.

Winry: Ed is the only person who managed to break the automail I made.

Edward: Well, It’s not like it’s on purpose.

Winry: Doesn’t matter! Don't go breaking it again. Otherwise, it looks like the work I do is cheap and shoddy.

Edward: You really shouldn’t put down your client, okay? 

Winry: And you really shouldn’t talk back!

Gracia: She's worried about you. You can tell how much danger Ed was in by the damage done to his automail, right?

Winry: No! I don't really worry about him...

Hughes: So, you’ve all been friends since you were kids?

Winry: Right. We were born in Resenbool and grew up there. We’ve known each other forever. We’re almost like siblings.  And now, how did you two of you meet each other?

Hughes: Cut to the chase. Get right to it. Okay, where do I begin?

Gracia: Once he gets started, he’ll go on forever. Let me get you a little more food.

Hughes: Don't get up. I'll do it. You need to take care of our little one.

Al?

Ed!

Al!

Huh?

What was I doing?

You!

Who are you?

I am what you call the world.

The universe.

God.

Truth.

One... and all.

I am also... you.

Here's what you wanted.

The truth.

My head hurts.

Don't destroy me.

No!

Please don't!

Mom.

Mom!

Well?

I understand now.

The truth.

I knew my theory was right.

I was so close to the truth of Human Transmutation!

Please let me see it once more.

Sorry, you can't.

You only have enough toll... to see that much.

Toll?

That's right.

Toll.

What?

Equivalent exchange.

Right?

Alchemist.

Al?

Al!

Damn it, he took my leg!

Somebody help me...

Mom...

Mom?

No...

This isn't what I wanted.

Al...

It's my fault.

Give back...

my brother.

Take my legs, arms, or heart!

But give him back...

He's the only family I have!

You're foolish... to return.

Not again...

The same nightmare?

It's always a reenactment... of that day.

Edward: The Sewing-Life Alchemist?

Mustang; That's right. His name is Shou Tucker. A pioneer in biological alchemy. Lately he’s been attempting to transmute Chimera with the ability to speak.

Alphonse: Chimera who can communicate? 

Winry: That I want to see!

Mustang: Two years ago, he transmuted a chimera. It said one thing. "Please kill me." And then it died.

Edward: Okay. But why are we meeting with him?

Mustang: General Hakuro told us to. He thinks he might have an idea about how to get Al’s body back.

Edward: This house is huge!

Winry: Ed!

Alphonse: Brother?

Nina: We have a lot of visitors, Dad!

Tucker: Hey Alexander!

Tucker: Yeah. Sorry about the mess. It's pretty much been like this since my wife left two years ago.

Edward: Thanks for seeing us. You must be busy.

Tucker: No problem.  It really is a privilege to meet the famous Fullmetal Alchemist. The pleasure is all mine. So, then, General Hakuro is behind your visit.  I will make you my top priority.

Edward: Thank you very much.

Nina: Al, this way!

Alphonse: Where are you? 

Winry: Here he comes!

Tucker: Nina is happy to have someone to play with. Sadly, I haven’t had much time lately.

Edward: Because your state evaluation is coming up.

Tucker: Honestly the progress reports I submit this year to Central Command really take a lot out of me. I'm certainly not a genius like you. It’s just that my credentials could be revoked this year. I just can’t let that happen.

Hughes: You think I’m not telling you why I'm here?

Mustang: If I'm being perfectly honest, yes. 

Hughes: Since the Ishvalan Civil war... things just haven’t been the same. The upheavals and riots have been relentless Suspecians rising among the citizens,

Mustang: You think I'm suspicious too.

Hughes: I’m not suspicious of you. But at central command. Some people are suspicious of what Eastern Headquarters is up to.

Mustang: So because we’re friends, they sent you here to keep an eye on me.

Hughes: Roy. If you want to get to the top, you’d better watch out for yourself. Not everyone in the state military will be supportive.

Mustang: Should I watch you?

Hughes: Yes, you should.

Edward: Whoa...

Tucker: They call me the pioneering creator of chimera, but really... Many of them are imperfect.

Edward: But you did manage to create a chimera that speaks.

Tucker: Yes, just once, two years ago.

Edward: You really did it. That’s so impressive.

Tucker: But... it only spoke three words and shortly after died.  But those three words were enough to earn my state alchemist credentials.

Alphonse: So, then, your mom's not around...?

Nina: But it's okay. I still have Dad and Alexander here with me.. Although. Lately, my Dad spends all his time in the lab. So it’s sometimes just me.

Winry: Well, now you have us to play with. As much as you want!

Nina: You sure? Yay, it will be fun, Alexander!

Edward: In the end, I managed to return to the Gate of Truth but all I was able to do was get back Al’s soul.

Tucker: So, you transmuted Al's soul... and you harnessed it in a suit of steel armor.

Edward: The price that I had to pay for returning Al’s soul was my right arm. But I.... I would have gladly given my life if it meant getting back Al’s body.

Tucker: It must have crushed you.

Edward: Mr. Tucker. Is it possible for you to get Al’s body back?

Tucker: I’m afraid it’s beyond my capabilities.

Edward: Yeah. I didn’t think so.

Tucker: Although... I’d like to do an experiment.

Hughes: Wait, Edward! I made it!

Edward: Sorry we had to leave so quickly.

Hughes: Yeah, I got caught up in some paperwork and I almost missed you. For you, Gracia made it.

Winry: Please tell her thank you for us.

Hughes: Hey, where’s Al?

Edward: Mr. Tucker wanted Al to stay there. He thinks he might be able to do something for him.

Hughes: That's good. So did you get some information from him?

Edward: He told us about a man named Doctor Marcoh, who’s been researching the Philosopher's Stone.

Winry: It seems that Dr. Marcoh disappeared a year ago, but recently, someone saw him in the Barns.

Ed, I hope that this time, you find the stone.

Tucker: My research wasn’t highly regarded last year. If I don’t do well this year, I’m at risk of revoking my state credentials.

Nina: You'll be okay. Cheer up, dad. Because you always study so hard. I love you, Dad.

Tucker: You just watch. I’ll pull it off.

Winry: You seemed worried. Don’t worry. Al's not a child anymore.

Edward: I'm not worried about him. It’s just... I’m not used to being without him.

Winry: I know. The two of you are always together.

Edward: Anyway, Mr. Tucker seems nice and Nina and Alexander are there.

Winry: That’s right. So cheer up. By the way, Nina seemed to really take a liking to you.

Ed, catch!

Ed, come and play with me!

Edward: Yeah, little kids always seemed to like me.

Winry: Right, because you’re their size.

[Winry opens the box to reveal an apple pie.]

Winry: Apple pie.

Edward: Are you gonna eat it already?

Winry: It’s okay. Gracia baked it for us to eat it. You know what I mean. Have a piece. 

Edward: Al couldn’t eat any of this even if he wanted to. He doesn’t sleep. He doesn’t feel pain or anything.

Winry: Remember Mr Tucker said he’d use hypnosis on Al, so he could study him and learn everything about him. It’ll just be like he’s sleeping. Maybe Al’s dreaming now.

Edward: You're right. In his dreams he’d be eating a huge apple pie all to himself.

Winry: Sure, sure! An extra large pie baked just for him.

Edward: A tall pie’s weird. Wouldn't it be sideways?

Winry: You think so? You want another slice? Have a tiny one.

Edward: Hey!

Alphonse: I feel like I’m hot all over...

Tucker: Maybe your soul is being drawn back into your body, Maybe or..

Alphonse: Or what?

Edward: It’s possible that your artificially implanted memories are just being harnessed.

Alphonse: Artificial implanted memories.

Tucker: It’s very rare for an alchemist to succeed in harnessing a living soul to an inmate object.

Alphonse: Are you suggesting that my memories are made up?

Tucker: It would be a logical explanation.... But you might also be a rare exception.

Alphonse: I feel dizzy... Artificial memories...

Edward: What?

Winry: We’re getting off!

Edward: What? Winry! Your bag! What’s going on? We’re not in the Barns yet.

Winry: The photo. Show me the photo Tucker gave you!

Edward: Here.

Winry: It's really him. Doctor Marcoh!

Edward: Winry! Wait up, Winry.

Winry: Hi, excuse me! Excuse me. Did you see this man just now?

No. Just a minute. Can I see that again?

Winry: Of course..

Ah, this is Dr. Mauro. He works in that hospital. I didn’t recognize him because he looks younger in the photo.

Edward: Excuse me. Dr. Marcoh, are you here?

Marco: Why are you here? How do you know my real name?

Winry: Please calm down, Dr. Marcoh.

Marco: I would never go back there.

Marco: I’m sorry about that. I thought, when I saw you, that they would come to take me back.

Edward: Who are they? I heard you did research on the Philosopher's Stone with the military. Is it something related to that? We didn’t come here to investigate for the military. Please. You have to tell me this one thing. Does the Philosopher's Stone really exist?

Marco: You should go now.

Edward: What?

Marco: With the shotgun, I saw you perform alchemy without a circle. You attempted human transmutation. And as a result, you accessed the Gate of Truth...

Edward: That's why I'm looking for the Philosopher's Stone.

Marco: Please believe me. You should stay away from the Stone for your own sake.

Edward: I need to find it, even if it costs my life!

Marco: Any involvement with the stone is playing with the devil. Pursuing it will land you in hell.

Edward: I've already been to hell.

Lust: How dare you. You actually killed me.

Winry: But how is... What is she?

Lust: Nice to see you again, Marcoh. So, you've been hiding out here all these years.

Marco: Lust. I’m not coming with you.

Lust: You won't have to, because you're no longer needed.

Edward: You witch!

Lust: Shut up!

Winry: Ed!

Edward: Winry! Winry!

Lust: Stop your squeaking... Fullmetal boy. If you can keep quiet, I’ll let you live. You'll make a fine sacrifice. But you, Marcoh, are worthless.

Edward: Don't kill him!

Winry: I'm okay.

Edward: Dr. Marcoh!

Marco: Keep this. It’s the circle..  To transmute... the Stone.

Edward: Did you actually transmute a philosopher stone?

Marco: Laboratory... Number 5.

Edward: Laboratory Number 5...

Winry: Dr. Marcoh? Doctor Marcoh?

Winry: Go. It’s fine.  I’ll take care of the mess and catch up with you after.

Edward: Winry.

Winry: Don’t worry. I’ve got it. I'll ask the local authorities for help. You finally got a good lead on the Philosopher’s stone. Be safe. Go make sure everything’s alright with Al.

Edward: The Philosopher's Stone... It exists!

Edward: Mr. Tucker! Hey, Al? Nina?

Tucker: Oh. Hi Ed.

Edward: I just got back. Mr. Tucker, I have something to tell you. Where’s Al?

Tucker: Don’t worry. He’ll wake up soon. I finally completed it. Look at it, Ed. A chimera who understands human language. Listen. That person is... Edward.

Ed... ..ward.

Tucker: That’s right. That is very good.

Edward: Wow. It can really speak!

Tucker: Yes. I managed to make it in time for the evaluation. Now the military will have no choice but to recognize my true talent. 

Edward. Edward! Ed, come and play with me!

Edward: Mr. Tucker.

Tucker: Yes?

Edward: Do you really think it’s impossible for you  to get Al’s body? When did you get the state credentials for your chimera research?

Tucker: It was two years ago.

Edward: When did you say when your wife left you?

Tucker: Two years ago.

Edward: One more question, if you don’t mind.  Nina and Alexander... Where are they?

Tucker: I hate insightful little brats like you.

Edward: Bastard! Two years ago, you used your wife. Now you transmuted another chimera using your dog and your own daughter! It makes sense. There’s only so much you can do with animals. But experimenting on humans?

Tucker: Why are you so upset? Without human experimentation mankind would have never progressed.

Edward: Have you completely lost your mind?! Do you think you’ll be forgiven for toying with human lives?!

Tucker: Toyed? That’s right. And what about the things that you did to you and your brother? You also toyed with human life. You and I, we're exactly the same.

Edward: Shut up!

Tucker: Fullmetal Alchemist, there's nothing that separates us.

Edward: Shut up!

Alphonse: Brother. If you keep that up, you’ll kill him. 

Tucker: Sometimes you gotta get your hands dirty.

Alphonse: Mr. Tucker. If you say another word, I’ll be the one to lose control.

Da... Dad...

Alphonse: Nina. I'm sorry. There’s no way to turn you back. We’re not capable of helping you.. We... can’t trust anyone anymore.

Play... with me. Play with me.

Mustang: We’re revolving your state alchemist credentials. Once at central, you'll be court-martialed. Take him away.

Riza: Do you think the two of them will be okay?

Tucker: Alphonse! You and I are so close to finding the truth!  See you soon!

Mustang: How long will you sit there and feel bad for yourself?

Edward: Leave me alone.

Mustang: You do realize that you are a State Alchemist. You joined to use that privilege to find a way to mend your and your brother’s bodies. And they call us devils. Don’t let something like this stop you.

Edward: Something like this. You’re right. I don’t care if they call me the military's dog or a devil. As long as Al and I get our bodies. But the thing is... we’re not really devils. We’re not gods either. We’re just humans. We’re just puny humans who can't do anything! Even save a little girl.

Mustang: Go back and rest before you catch a cold.

Alphonse: So Ed’s been locked in the library for three days now. He's hardly eaten anything.

Winry: Right...

Alphonse: He won't let me help him. He says that it's too dangerous. What happened when you two were away? 

Hughes: What are you doing in this dungeon all by yourself?

Edward: Captain Hughes...

Hughes: So, did you find what you're looking for? Why do this alone? We are friends aren’t we? Hey Ed! Aren’t we friends?

Edward: Of course.

Hughes: And friends help each other out. Please come in. Meet Lieutenant Ross and sandwiches! Eat some or you'll collapse.

Edward: Thank you but she works for Colonel Mustang.

Hughes: She's a highly trusted officer. She’s also a good friend. Can’t go wrong with friends of friends, right? I said, can’t go wrong with friends.

Edward: I heard you.

Hughes: Eat something. Then we’ll talk about the transmutation circle and Laboratory Number 5.

Edward: What? Winry, I told her not to tell anyone.

Hughes: You can’t blame her. I'm very persuasive.

Edward: But... This all falls under the category of classified information. Dr. Marcoh was murdered. Anyone involved runs that risk.

Hughes: We must not have heard. I’ll say it again. We are friends.

Hughes: I don’t know.  We haven’t found anything to Marcoh’s transmutation circle for the Philosopher's Stone. 

Ross: It seems like they have destroyed any of Marcohs remaining documents. 

Hughes: Question is.. Who’s behind it and why did they do it.

Ross: It’s looking to me like an inside job. 

Hughes: I think you’re probably right.

Ross: The military, stone,

Hughes: And the mysterious woman with the tattoo.

Edward: She's not  human. I can’t believe that woman is human. What about Laboratory Number 5?

Ross: Marcoh belonged to a lab but it was Laboratory Number 3. There’s a total of four official military laboratories. But there’s no record anywhere of Laboratory Number 5.

Edward: But I’m sure I heard him say Lab Number 5!

Hakuro: Is this a special meeting? 

Edward: General Hakuro!

Hakuro: Hughes is here too.

Hughes: We are... looking....

Hakuro: That’s right. That was what a select group of people used to call it. 

Hughes: Sir?

Hakuro: I’m talking about your lab. Laboratory 5.

Edward: Do you know where it is?

Hakuro: It was before I was stationed here. I think it's this one.

Edward: It’s a cannery.

Hughes: Why did they call a cannery Laboratory Number 5?

Ross: The cannery is not operating. It's in ruins now.

Edward: Thank you very much, General!

Hughes: Ed!

General! Emergency. It’s Reole sir. Full scale riots are underway.

Hughes: Reole? Where the sham priest was operating.

Sir, there has been a report of casualties.

Hakuro: I’ll leave now. Lieutenant Ross, return to your station.

Ross: Sir. 

Hakuro: You’re our guest, Captain. I’m in command at Reole. Mustang will need to be at headquarters, leaving you to stay here and help out. There’s already been so much bloody violence in this country. First in Ishval, now it’s Reole.

Alphonse: : This is the last one.

Winry: There's no sign of a laboratory left.

Edward: Damn! Damn! I know we're so close to the secret of the Philosopher's Stone. Cheer up, Al.We;re really close to the secret. Once we discover it, we’ll get your body back.

Alphonse: Tell me what happened when you were with Dr. Marcoh.

Winry: Al...

Alphonse: Is there something that you’ve been keeping from me? 

Edward: No.

Alphonse: We don't even know for sure if my body actually exists. How can you be so sure that we’re ever going to be able to get it back? You say you transmuted my soul. But maybe it’s not really a soul. Maybe it;s just some made-up memory you created.

Edward: What are you talking about?

Alphonse: Tucker told me that transmuting a soul and harnessing within an object would be virtually impossible, especially for a kid. 

Edward: That bastard Tucker.  Whatever that lunatic said to you, he wasn’t telling you the truth. You have to trust me.

Alphonse: I have to trust you? Look at me, I’m hollow! How can I trust anyone!? Maybe I never even existed.

Edward: Al...

Alphonse: Maybe everything is made up! I know you're all deceiving me. Ed, you’re a liar!

Winry: Ed!

Alphonse: No. Maybe you’re not even my real brother.

Winry: What do you think you’re doing? Stop it, you two!

Alphonse: Brother, use your right hand. You’re going to ruin your left one.

Winry: Al, you idiot! Ed cares about you more than anything. Ed is always, constantly thinking about you. Even when it's just the two of us all he does is talk about you.

Alphonse: Winry...

Winry: He believes it's entirely his fault you lost your body.  He always blames himself. Don’t you know that’s the reason he does all those dangerous things to get your body back? Hey! What idiot would risk his life just to create a fake brother to torment himself with. The two of you... are the only thing the other one has!

Edward: Al. I’m not finished!

Alphonse: Brother... It hurts.

Edward: Liar! You don't feel pain. 

Alphonse: Brother. It hurts to see you hurt.

Edward: I promise. I'll get your body back. Trust me.

Hughes: The Ishvalan Civil war. And riots in Reole. There were riots in the north and west as well as border conflicts. Oh God. Hold on a second. Now think. Who’s behind it? Wait a minute... That’s what I thought. The position of the four laboratories is strategic. So it makes sense for number 5 to be... There you are.

Lust: Now you know too much.

Hughes: The Ouroboros tattoo.  You’re the one who attacked and killed Dr. Marcoh.

Lust: Nice to meet you. Or is "Goodbye" more appropriate?

Lust: I mistook him for the brainy type. That was a good fight captain.

I’m calling from outside because the internal lines aren't secure.

May I have the security code?
